description: Master data file for the 90-session catechetical formation track. Each session is one day's reading. Pius X Q ranges are inclusive on both ends. Aquinas and Trent chapter IDs reference catechetical-instructions and catechism-of-trent in the same library. teacher = 'aquinas' means the Aquinas chapter is the primary pastoral exposition; teacher = 'trent' means Trent fills that role (Aquinas has no parallel sermon).

title.en-US: Who Made Us, Who God Is
title.pt-BR: Quem nos criou, quem é Deus

sessions.image_topic: God the Creator separating light from darkness; or Genesis creation scene

title.en-US: Heaven, Hell, and Why We Are Here
title.pt-BR: O Céu, o inferno, e por que estamos aqui

sessions.image_topic: Christ the Teacher with disciples, or the Last Judgment showing heaven and hell
Apostle (St. Peter or St. Paul) writing or preaching the Gospel; or Pentecost
Crucifixion (simple iconic form, e.g. Cimabue, El Greco, or a Russian icon of the Crucifixion)
Moses and the Burning Bush (the 'I AM' moment, e.g. Chagall or a Greek icon)

sections: I Believe in the Holy Ghost

sessions.image_topic: The Holy Trinity — Andrei Rublev's Trinity icon (Three Angels at Mamre); or Masaccio's Trinity

title.en-US: Three Persons, One God, Equal and Eternal
title.pt-BR: Três Pessoas, um só Deus, iguais e eternos

sections: Certain Divine Works are Appropriated to the Holy Ghost

sessions.image_topic: Coronation of the Virgin showing the Trinity (e.g. Velázquez's Coronation, or Fra Angelico)

title.pt-BR: Criador do Céu e da Terra

sessions.image_topic: Creation of the world (Michelangelo's Sistine Creation panels, or William Blake)

sections: Of all Things Visible and Invisible

sessions.image_topic: St. Michael casting down the devil (Reni, Raphael, or a medieval icon); or the Guardian Angel (Pietro da Cortona)

sections: Errors Relating to the Second Article

sections: Duties Owed To Christ Our Lord
